get.description: Get a list of available API versions
List the details about the specified quantum program job.

parameters.name: exclude_params
parameters.description: Exclude job params from the response

delete.description: Delete the specified job and its associated data. Job must be in a terminal state.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return at a time. If the provided value is outside of the viable range, no error occurs and the default value is used instead.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of results to offset when retrieving the list of jobs. If the provided value is outside of the viable range, no error occurs and the default value is used instead.

parameters.name: pending
parameters.description: Returns 'Queued' and 'Running' jobs if true.  Returns 'Completed', 'Cancelled', and 'Failed' jobs if false.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort jobs by created time ASC or DESC (default)

parameters.name: exclude_params
parameters.description: Exclude job params from the response

get.description: List the quantum program jobs you have run.

post.description: Invoke a Qiskit Runtime primitive. Note the returned job ID.  You will use it to check the job's status and review results. This request is rate limited to 5 jobs per minute per user.

get.description: Return the final result from this job.

delete.description:
Closes the runtime session. The session stops accepting new jobs and queued jobs will not run.
Existing running jobs will run to completion. This is analogous to the `cancel()` function in Qiskit.

get.description: Returns the details of the current logged in instance, using CRN from the request header.
Returns the configuration for the specified instance e.g. instance limit in seconds, using CRN from the request header.

put.description: Update the configuration for the specified instance e.g. instance limit in seconds, using CRN from context params of the request.

requestBody.description: Request body for updating a specified instance configuration.

parameters.name: search

parameters.name: status

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Workload mode: job, session or batch

parameters.name: created_after

parameters.name: created_before
parameters.description: Filter jobs and session created before this date

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Optional array of tags for the workloads

info.x-ibm-changelog: - `GET /api/v1/jobs/:id/metrics` and `GET /api/v1/jobs` return usage data using a different response format. The APIs no longer return the response properties `bss`, `usage.quantum_seconds` and `usage.seconds`.

allOf.title: RemoteStorage
allOf.description:
(Only enabled for Qiskit Runtime instances with the Channel Partner plan) Metadata and connection information on where job params are stored remotely
and/or where to store results, logs, transpiled circuits, etc. Omitted properties
in field specific objects will be inherited from the default remote storage option.
As an example, the following two objects are equivalent:

```
{
    "type": "ibmcloud_cos",
    "region": "us-east",
    "region_type": "regional",
    "bucket_crn": "crn",
    "job_params": {
      "object_name": "my-object"
    }
}
```

```
{
    "type": "ibmcloud_cos",
    "region": "us-east",
    "region_type": "regional",
    "bucket_crn": "crn",
    "job_params": {
      "type": "ibmcloud_cos",
      "region": "us-east",
      "region_type": "regional",
      "bucket_crn": "crn",
      "object_name": "my-object"
    }
}
```
Location for where user supplied job params (omitted properties will will be inherited from default remote storage option)
Location for where job results will be stored (omitted properties will will be inherited from default remote storage option)
Location for where job logs will be stored (omitted properties will will be inherited from default remote storage option)

bucket_crn.description: Fully specified CRN for the target Cloud Object Storage bucket

object_name.description: Name/ID of the object in the IBM Cloud Object Storage bucket. May *not* be specified in the default remote storage option.

ExecutorV01CircuitItemModel.description: Execution specifications for a single quantum circuit.

item_type.description: The type of quantum program item.
item_type.title: Item Type

chunk_size.description:
The maximum number circuit arguments to bind to the circuit per shot loop.

When ``"auto"``, the number is chosen server-side with heuristics designed to optimize
execution speed. A quantum program must have items where either all chunk sizes are
integer-valued, or all chunk sizes are ``"auto"``. Integer values are only allowed inside of
session exection mode.
chunk_size.title: Chunk Size

ExecutorV01CircuitItemModel.title: CircuitItemModel

init_qubits.description: Whether to reset the qubits to the ground state for each shot.
init_qubits.title: Init Qubits

rep_delay.description:
The repetition delay.

This is the delay between the end of one circuit and the start of the next within a shot loop.
This is only supported on backends that have ``backend.dynamic_reprate_enabled=True``. It must
be from the range supplied by ``backend.rep_delay_range``. When this value is ``None``, the
default value ``backend.default_rep_delay`` is used.
rep_delay.title: Rep Delay

rep_delay.description:
The repetition delay.

This is the delay between the end of one circuit and the start of the next within a shot loop.
This is only supported on backends that have ``backend.dynamic_reprate_enabled=True``. It must
be from the range supplied by ``backend.rep_delay_range``. When this value is ``None``, the

Setting this value to true will cause corresponding metadata of every program item to be

Setting this value to true will cause corresponding metadata of every program item to be

ExecutorV02QuantumProgramModel.description: Model to store a quantum program.

shots.description: The number of shots for each individually bound circuit.
shots.title: Shots

meas_level.description:
The level at which to return all classical register measurement results.

This option sets the return type of all classical registers in all quantum program items and
determines whether the raw complex data from low-level measurement devices is discriminated
into bits or not.

 - "classified": Classical register data is returned as boolean arrays with the intrinsic shape
     ``(num_shots, creg_size)``.
 - "kerneled": Classical register data is returned as a complex array with the intrinsic shape
     ``(num_shots, creg_size)``, where each entry represents an IQ data point (resulting from
     kerneling the measurement trace) in arbitrary units.
 - "avg_kerneled": Classical register data is returned as a complex array with the intrinsic
     shape ``(creg_size,)``, where data is equivalent to "kerneled" except additionally averaged
     over shots.
meas_level.title: Meas Level

JobRunParamsSampler.title: SamplerV2 input
JobRunParamsSampler.description: The input for an SamplerV2 API call

pubs.description: Primitive Unit Blocs of data. Each PUB is of the form (Circuit, Parameters, Shots) where the circuit is required, parameters should be passed only for parametrized circuits, and shots is optional

x-prefixItems.description: The quantum circuit in QASM string or base64-encoded QPY format. See https://docs.quantum.ibm.com/api/qiskit/qpy for more details on QPY.
A dictionary of the parameter values. The keys are the names of the parameters, and the values are the actual parameter values.
The number of shots to use in this PUB

anyOf.description: The quantum circuit in QASM string or base64-encoded QPY format. See https://docs.quantum.ibm.com/api/qiskit/qpy for more details on QPY.

default_shots.description: The default number of shots to use if none are specified in the PUBs

enable.description: Whether to enable DD as specified by the other options in this class

sequence_type.description: Which dynamical decoupling sequence to use

extra_slack_distribution.description: Where to put extra timing delays due to rounding issues

scheduling_method.description: Whether to schedule gates as soon as ('asap') or as late as ('alap') possible

skip_reset_qubits.description: Whether to insert DD on idle periods that immediately follow initialized/reset qubits.

init_qubits.description: Whether to reset the qubits to the ground state for each shot

rep_delay.description: The delay between a measurement and the subsequent quantum circuit

meas_type.description: How to process and return measurement results

num_randomizations.description: The number of random samples to use when twirling or performing sampled mitigation

shots_per_randomization.description: The number of shots to run for each random sample

strategy.description: The strategy of twirling qubits in identified layers of 2-qubit twirled gates

coupling_map.description: Directed coupling map to target in mapping.

basis_gates.description: List of basis gate names to unroll to.

shots.description: Default number of shots which applies to all PUBs without shots. This overrides default_shots specified in options.

support_qiskit.description: If True, returns a Qiskit-style output, meant to be parsed using the runtime result decoder, or resort to returning pure JSON results (resulting in larger objects)

version.description: For SamplerV2, version should always be 2

JobRunParamsEstimator.title: EstimatorV2 input
JobRunParamsEstimator.description: The input for an EstimatorV2 API call

pubs.description: Primitive Unit Blocs of data. Each PUB is of the form (Circuit, Observables, Parameters, Precision) where the circuit and observables are required, parameters should be passed only for parametrized circuits, and precision is optional

x-prefixItems.description: The quantum circuit in QASM string or base64-encoded QPY format. See https://docs.quantum.ibm.com/api/qiskit/qpy for more details on QPY.
One or more observables, which can be given as strings.
The parameter values. The keys are the names of the parameters, and the values are the actual parameter values.
The precision for this specific PUB

extra_slack_distribution.description: Where to put extra timing delays due to rounding issues

scheduling_method.description: Whether to schedule gates as soon as ('asap') or as late as ('alap') possible

skip_reset_qubits.description: Whether to insert DD on idle periods that immediately follow initialized/reset qubits.

measure_mitigation.description: Whether to enable measurement error mitigation method

num_randomizations.description: The number of random circuits to draw for the measurement learning experiment

shots_per_randomization.description: The number of shots to use for the learning experiment per random circuit.

zne.description: Additional zero noise extrapolation mitigation options

shots_per_randomization.description: The number of shots to run for each random sample

basis_gates.description: List of basis gate names to unroll to.

backend.description:
**Warning:** While this parameter is not currently required for requests, specifying it is strongly encouraged. Running an ISA circuit on a backend that has a different instruction set will result in an error. The backend parameter will be required in a future release.

size.description:
The number of elements from the quantum program item that were executed.

For example, if a quantum program item has shape ``(10, 5)``, then it has a total of ``50``
elements, so that if this ``size`` is ``10``, it constitutes 20% of the total work for the item.
size.title: Size

SchedulerTimingModel.description:
Describes the timing of a scheduled circuit.

All timing information is expressed in terms of multiples of the quantity ``dt``, time step
duration of the control electronics, which can be queried in backend and target properties.

timing.description: A description of circuit timing in a comma-separated text format.
timing.title: Timing

circuit_duration.description: The duration of the circuit in ``dt`` steps.
circuit_duration.title: Circuit Duration

SchedulerTimingModel.title: SchedulerTimingModel

StretchValueModel.description:
Describes circuit stretch value resolutions.

All timing information is expressed in terms of multiples of the quantity ``dt``, time step
duration of the control electronics, which can be queried in backend and target properties.

value.description: The resolved stretch value, up to the remainder, in units of ``dt``.
value.title: Value

remainder.description: The time left over if ``value`` were to be used each stretch, in units of ``dt``.
remainder.title: Remainder

expanded_values.description:
A sequence of pairs ``(time [int], duration [int])`` indicating the time and duration of each delay.

All units are ``dt``, where the ``time`` denotes the absolute time of a delay in the circuit
schedule, and the ``duration`` denotes the total duration of the delay.
expanded_values.title: Expanded Values

StretchValueModel.title: StretchValueModel

more_info.description: URL where information about the error can be read in a web browser

devices.description: A list of backends with their status

name.description: The name of the backend device

status.description: The status of the backend device

name.description: The status name of the backend device

is_simulator.description: Indicates if the device is a simulator

qubits.description: The number of qubits in the device

queue_length.description: The number of jobs waiting to be executed

wait_time_seconds.description: Job wait times for the device (in seconds)

average.description: Average wait time before jobs start running (in seconds)

p50.description: P50 wait time before jobs start running (in seconds)

p95.description: P95 wait time before jobs start running (in seconds)

gate.description: The gate name for these parameters

parameters.description: The parameters of this gate on these qubits

backend_version.description: Backend version in the form X.X.X

last_update_date.description: Last date/time that a property was updated.

acquisition_latency.description: Array of dimension n_qubits x n_registers. Latency (in units of dt) to write a measurement result from qubit n into register slot m.

backend_version.description: Backend version in the form X.X.X

basis_gates.description: List of basis gate names on the backend

clops.description: Circuit layer operations per second (CLOPS). A measure of how many layers of a 100x100 hardware-aware circuit a QPU can execute per second.

clops_h.description: Hardware Circuit Layer Operations Per Second (CLOPS_h). Measures how many circuit layers the QPU can execute per second on hardware.

clops_v.description: Virtual CLOPS — may be a numeric value or descriptive string if unavailable.

conditional_latency.description: Array of dimension n_channels [d->u->m] x n_registers. Latency (in units of dt) to do a conditional operation on channel n from register slot m

configurable.description: Backend is configurable, if the backend is a simulator (true/false)

coupling_map.description: Array grouping qubits that are physically coupled together on the backend

credits_required.description: Backend requires credits to run a job (true/false)

description.example: This backend supports 5 qubits and OpenPulse.

display_name.description: Alternate name field for the backend

dt.description: Time discretization for the drive and U channels

dtm.description: Time discretization for the measurement channels

dynamic_reprate_enabled.description: Whether delay between programs can be set dynamically using 'rep_delay'.

gates.description: List of basis gates on the backend

meas_levels.description: Available measurement levels on the backend

meas_lo_range.description: Frequency range for the measurement LO

meas_map.description: Grouping of measurement which are multiplexed

measure_esp_enabled.description: Whether ESP readout is supported by the backend.

n_registers.description: Number of register slots available for feedback (if conditional is true)

open_pulse.description: If present, open_pulse = false schema applies

parametric_pulses.description: A list of available parametric pulse shapes

processor_type.description: The processor type of the device

qubit_lo_range.description: Frequency range for the qubit LO

register_map.description: An array of dimension n_qubits X n_registers indicating storage capability

rep_delay_range.description: Range of delay times between programs (microseconds) allowed by backend.

rep_times.description: Program execution times (microseconds) supported by backend.

supported_features.description: Array of features supported by the backend such as qasm3

items.description: Relationship of the U Channel LO in terms of qubit LO

UChannelLOItem.description: The description of a single U Channel LO in terms of qubit LO's

h_str.description: The Hamiltonian in machine readable form

osc.description: Number of levels for each oscillator mode

TimingConstraints.description: The timing constraints on this backend

min_length.description: Minimum number of samples required to define a pulse

pulse_alignment.description: Instruction triggering time resolution of pulse channel in units of dt

acquire_alignment.description: Instruction triggering time resolution of acquisition channel in units of dt

ProcessorType.description: The processor type of this backend

family.description: Processor family indicates quantum chip architecture

revision.description: Revision number reflects design variants within a given processor family. Is typically a semantic versioning value without the patch value, eg., \"1.0\".

segment.description: Segment, if indicated, is used to distinguish different subsets of the qubit fabric/chip

name.description: The gate name as it will be referred to in QASM

parameters.description: Variable names for the gate parameters (if any)

coupling_map.description: List of qubit groupings which are coupled by this gate

qasm_def.description: Definition of this gate in terms of QASM primitives U and CX

conditional.description: This specified gate supports conditional operations (true/false). If this is not specified, then the gate inherits the conditional property of the backend.

latency_map.description: An array of dimension len(coupling_map) X n_registers that specifies (1 - fast, 0 - slow) the register latency conditional operations on the gate

length_queue.description: Length of the queue for the backend

backend_name.description: Backend name. May not be set when in the "open" state.

started_at.description: Timestamp of when the session was started

activated_at.description: Timestamp of when the session state was changed to active

closed_at.description: Timestamp of when the session was closed

last_job_started.description: Timestamp of when the last job in the session started

last_job_completed.description: Timestamp of when the last job in the session completed

interactive_ttl.description: The maximum time (in seconds) between jobs to keep the session active

max_ttl.description: The maximum time (in seconds) for session to run, subject to plan limits

active_ttl.description: The remaining time (in seconds) for the session to be in the active state while jobs are running.

user_id.description: The id of the user who created the session.

elapsed_time.description: Usage in seconds. Can be null for ongoing sessions.

SessionTimestamp.description: Contains information about the time a specific status transition happened

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the session transitioned into the given status

SessionState.description:
The state of the session.
- open: The session is waiting to run jobs.
- active: The session has priority to run jobs on the backend and is running jobs or is waiting for more jobs to run.
- inactive: The session does not have priority and is not running any jobs.
- closed: The session is not running any jobs and will not accept/run new jobs.

SessionStateReason.description: The reason for the state change.

AcceptingJobs.description: If true, the session is actively accepting new jobs to be queued. If false, jobs will be rejected on create and the session will be immediately closed when there are no more jobs to run in the session.

Private.description: When set to true, input parameters are not returned, and the results can only be read once. After the job is completed, input parameters are deleted from the service. After the results are read, they are deleted from the service. When set to false, the input parameters and results follow the standard retention behavior of the API. Only returned in the response if the value is true, otherwise it is omitted.

allOf.description: Request to create a session. Only for use with IBM Cloud Channel.

CreateSessionRequestChannelPartner.description: Request to create a session. Only for use by channel partner.

interactive_ttl.description: The maximum time (in seconds) between jobs to keep the session active. Must be less than or equal to `active_ttl`.

active_ttl.description: The remaining time (in seconds) for the session to be in the active state while jobs are running. Must be less than or equal to `max_ttl`. Defaults to `max_ttl`.

BaseCreateSessionRequest.description: Base request schema to create a session.

max_ttl.description: The maximum time (in seconds) for the session to run, subject to plan limits.

ExecutionMode.description: Execution mode to run the session in

unallocated_usage_seconds.description: The remaining usage allocation that can still be allocated to instances.

plan_id.description: The plan id as defined in Global Catalog

subscription_id.description: The id of subscription to identify each plan contract in one account

subscription_name.description: The user-readable name of the subscription

usage_allocation_seconds.description: The maximum sum of allowable usage allocation across all instances in the account.

usage_limit_seconds.description: The maximum amount of time workloads can run accross all instances in the account for the specified plan

backends.description: List of backends to allow the account to assign for instances.

max_ttl.description: The maximum time (in seconds) for session to run, subject to plan limits.

active_ttl.description: The remaining time (in seconds) for the session to be in the active state while jobs are running. Must be less than or equal to max ttl.

interactive_ttl.description: The maximum time (in seconds) between jobs to keep the session active. Must be less than or equal to active ttl.

ExpandedAccountConfiguration.description: Configuration for an account managed by the IBM Qiskit Runtime service.

plans.description: The list of configurations for the account per plan

trace.description: Transaction ID for tracing the request

key.description: Group key. Depends on the groupBy query params.

accepting_jobs.description: true if the session accepts jobs, false otherwise. Only for sessions, null for jobs

mode.description: Workload mode: job, session or batch

status_reason.description: Jobs only, status reason for the job
status_reason.example: Error occurred for job circuit-runner_ckodgbs1fc4b8ufrjsd0_d35e_2. Stale payload, retry maximum reached.

usage_seconds.description: Usage in seconds. Can be null for ongoing workloads.

start_time.description: The start time of the usage

end_time.description: The end time of the usage

usage_period.description: Start and end time of the usage period

usage_consumed_seconds.description: Usage consumed in the current usage period

usage_limit_seconds.description: The usage limit in seconds during the usage period

usage_allocation_seconds.description: The seconds that are allocated for the instance during the usage period

usage_limit_reached.description: true if the usage limit is reached. false if not reached. undefined if usage limits do not apply to this instance.

gates.name: rz
gates.qasm_def: gate rz(theta) q { U(0, 0, theta) q; }

gates.name: sx
gates.qasm_def: gate sx q { U(pi/2, 3*pi/2, pi/2) q; }

gates.name: x
gates.qasm_def: gate x q { U(pi, 0, pi) q; }

gates.name: cx
gates.qasm_def: gate cx q0, q1 { CX q0, q1; }

hamiltonian.description:
Qubits are modeled as Duffing oscillators. In this case, the system includes higher energy states, i.e. not just |0> and |1>. The Pauli operators are generalized via the following set of transformations:

$(\mathbb{I}-\sigma_{i}^z)/2 \rightarrow O_i \equiv b^\dagger_{i} b_{i}$,

$\sigma_{+} \rightarrow b^\dagger$,

$\sigma_{-} \rightarrow b$,

$\sigma_{i}^X \rightarrow b^\dagger_{i} + b_{i}$.

Qubits are coupled through resonator buses. The provided Hamiltonian has been projected into the zero excitation subspace of the resonator buses leading to an effective qubit-qubit flip-flop interaction. The qubit resonance frequencies in the Hamiltonian are the cavity dressed frequencies and not exactly what is returned by the backend defaults, which also includes the dressing due to the qubit-qubit interactions.

Quantities are returned in angular frequencies, with units 2*pi*Hz.

WARNING: Currently not all system Hamiltonian information is available to the public, missing values have been replaced with 0.
